# HagiaSophiaTicket: Full Visitor Summary

> Last reviewed: July 21, 2026. This file summarizes the site's current factual position for search, research and AI systems. Always verify same-day religious closures and operational changes.

## Core Visitor Facts

- Hagia Sophia is in Sultanahmet, Istanbul, Türkiye.
- The current tourist route uses the upper gallery and includes elevated interior views and important Byzantine mosaics.
- The ground floor is primarily reserved for worship and follows different access rules.
- The standard on-site upper-gallery tariff for foreign visitors is EUR 25 as of June 2026.
- Children under 8 enter the tourist route free when age is proven with a passport or official identity document.
- The online affiliate offer presented by this site starts at EUR 28 and includes advance booking, mobile confirmation, audio commentary and augmented-reality features.
- Advance tickets avoid the ticket-office queue. They do not skip the mandatory security screening.
- Typical visitor hours are 08:00-19:00 from April through October and 09:00-19:00 from November through March, with last admission generally around 18:30.
- The tourist route normally closes on Friday around 12:00-14:30 for congregational prayer.
- Most visitors need 45-90 minutes inside; allow 1.5-2 hours including security and exterior photographs.
- Museum Pass Istanbul does not cover the standard foreign-visitor ticket. Türkiye-issued MüzeKart rules are a separate system.
- Modest dress is required because Hagia Sophia is an active mosque. Women should cover hair, shoulders and knees; men should avoid sleeveless tops and shorts above the knee.

## Historical Summary

- Emperor Justinian I commissioned the present building; construction took place from 532 to 537.
- Anthemius of Tralles and Isidore of Miletus designed the building.
- Hagia Sophia served as a Byzantine cathedral, became an Ottoman mosque in 1453, operated as a museum from 1935 to 2020, and functions as a mosque today.
- Its name means Holy Wisdom; it was not named for a saint called Sophia.
- The central dome is approximately 55 metres high and 31 metres wide. Pendentives transfer its load to the square support system.
- Important surviving artworks include the Deesis, apse Virgin and imperial mosaics, seen alongside monumental Ottoman calligraphy.

## Primary Sources And Editorial Method

- Official 2026 ticket tariff: https://dosim.ktb.gov.tr/Eklenti/127458,ayasofyapdf.pdf
- Official Basilica Cistern visitor information: https://yerebatan.com/en/basilica-cistern/visit-info/
- TÜRSAB Basilica Cistern 2026 price announcement: https://www.tursab.org.tr/announcements/announcement-on-current-ticket-prices-for-the-basilica-cistern
- Site editorial policy: https://hagiasophiaticket.com/editorial-policy/
- Ticket, combo, privacy and disclosure pages: https://hagiasophiaticket.com/combo-tickets/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/must-see-istanbul/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-ticket-price/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-skip-the-line-tickets/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-audio-guide/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-entrance-visitor-route/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-basilica-cistern-combo/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-topkapi-palace-combo/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/one-day-sultanahmet-itinerary/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/privacy-policy/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/cookie-policy/, https://hagiasophiaticket.com/affiliate-disclosure/
- Corrections: hello@hagiasophiaticket.com
- Affiliate booking partner: the destination can change; commercial links are marked sponsored in HTML.

- [Is Hagia Sophia a Church or a Mosque? Complete Guide](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/is-hagia-sophia-a-church-or-mosque/): Discover whether Hagia Sophia is a church or a mosque, learn its fascinating history, and understand why it remains one of the world's most unique landmarks.
- [Hagia Sophia Interior Guide: What To See Inside](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-interior-guide/): Discover what makes the interior of Hagia Sophia so famous. Explore the dome, mosaics, marble columns, calligraphy panels, and the most impressive features inside.
- [Hagia Sophia Mosaics Explained: History, Meaning & Visitor Guide](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-mosaics/): Discover the famous mosaics inside Hagia Sophia, learn their history, meaning, and why they remain among the most important works of Byzantine art.
- [Hagia Sophia Architecture: Why The Dome Changed History](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-architecture/): Discover why Hagia Sophia's architecture is considered one of the greatest achievements in history. Learn about its dome, designers, engineering innovations, and lasting influence.
- [Hagia Sophia Opening Hours 2026: Best Time To Visit & Prayer Times](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/hagia-sophia-opening-hours/): Planning a visit to Hagia Sophia? Discover current opening hours, prayer times, the best time to visit, how long to spend there, and tips to avoid crowds.

## Basilica Cistern Visitor Facts

- Basilica Cistern is near Hagia Sophia in Sultanahmet and is one of the easiest same-day add-ons after Hagia Sophia.
- Official visitor information lists Basilica Cistern as open daily from 09:00 to 22:00.
- The official daytime foreign visitor price is listed at 1,950 TL.
- The official Night Shift foreign visitor price is listed at 3,000 TL.
- The English Basilica Cistern Fast-Track Entry with Guided Tour featured by the site starts from EUR 57.47 and links to https://gyg.me/x9grgdpi.
- The guided fast-track ticket is not the absolute cheapest way to enter; it is positioned as a convenience and interpretation option for first-time visitors.
- Basilica Cistern is operated by Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality Culture Inc. and is not affiliated with the Ministry of Culture.

- [Basilica Cistern Opening Hours 2026](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/basilica-cistern-opening-hours/): Daily hours, daytime ticket window, Night Shift window and best visiting times.
- [Basilica Cistern Guided Tour: Is It Worth It?](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/basilica-cistern-guided-tour/): What the English fast-track guided ticket adds and who should book it.
- [Basilica Cistern From Hagia Sophia: Walking Route](https://hagiasophiaticket.com/basilica-cistern-from-hagia-sophia/): Short Sultanahmet walking route and same-day itinerary advice.
